$comment: Single source of truth for hd-config.md schema. detect.py imports SCHEMA_VERSION + REQUIRED_FIELDS from this file. References/hd-config-schema.md is the human doc derived from this spec; if they drift, this file wins. Bump major version (integer) only on breaking changes; additive fields don't bump (K8s / dbt convention).
title: hd-config.md schema

additive_changes_since_lock.note: Universal tool discovery — Layer A enumeration; categorization happens at research time via ai-integration-scout
Generic root-level *.md probe (≥30 non-blank lines) for external formats like DESIGN.md / CONTRIBUTING.md — no filename whitelist

schema_version.note: Quoted string, not bare int. K8s/dbt convention.

team_tooling.note: v5 added cli + data_api categories (3n.7); 3o removed CATEGORY_PATTERNS — categorization now happens at research time via scout

urls.note: Deduped external URL list filtered for noise

validation_rules: YAML frontmatter parses as valid YAML
schema_version present and string-typed (not bare number)
setup_mode is one of the 4 enum values
setup_date matches YYYY-MM-DD (10 chars, ISO date)
team_size is one of the 4 enum values
If layer_decisions present, each entry has `layer`, `decision`, and `files_written` keys
If other_tool_harnesses_detected present, each entry has `name`, `type`, and `paths_found` keys
Missing optional fields default to: skipped_layers=[], article_read=false, layer_decisions=[], other_tool_harnesses_detected=[], files_written=[]

additive_no_bump: Add new optional sub-field on existing object
Add new enum value (e.g., new team_tooling category)
version_bump_policy.convention: K8s / dbt — additive changes don't bump; breaking changes do

consumers: skills/hd-setup/scripts/detect.py — imports SCHEMA_VERSION at module init; emits in JSON output
skills/hd-setup/references/hd-config-schema.md — human-readable derivation; cite this file as authoritative
skills/hd-setup/assets/hd-config.md.template — example values; update when schema changes
skills/hd-review/scripts/budget-check.sh — does not consume schema, but reads hd-config.md alongside
Any /hd:* skill that reads hd-config.md — must tolerate unknown top-level fields per backward-compat contract
